nodes.label: Atelier (kanban-agents project)
nodes.rationale: Local single-user kanban orchestrating autonomous Claude Code sessions that implement tickets end-to-end; the board routes work, the backend verifies gates, the agents do the reasoning.

nodes.label: Ticket
nodes.rationale: One unit of work the board tracks; carries Kind, Column, Stage and agent knobs. All mutations go through store.ts.

nodes.label: Kind (ticket pipeline type)
nodes.rationale: What pipeline a ticket runs: feature, review, clean, ask. Conflict resolution is a mode on a feature ticket, not a kind.

nodes.label: Column (board lane)
nodes.rationale: The coarse, user-visible board lane axis (8: todo, implementing, prd, done, merged, reviewed, failed, abandoned).

nodes.label: Stage (pipeline state)
nodes.rationale: Fine-grained pipeline state, a different axis from Column (12 stages). Use ACTIVE_STAGES/TERMINAL_STAGES, never hardcode. Subset is agent-settable; terminal stages are backend-set only.

nodes.label: Slot (git-worktree execution unit)
nodes.rationale: A git-worktree execution unit from a fixed pool (SLOT_COUNT) at SLOTS_ROOT/slot-N. Git ops per repo serialize through repoMutex.

nodes.label: SlotManager
nodes.rationale: Owns slot lifecycle: cleanup -> fetch -> worktree add -> install -> start SDK session -> inject contract -> done gate -> release. No free slot -> FIFO queue.

nodes.label: SessionHub
nodes.rationale: Owns each live SDK agent session (query() streaming-input, in-process). Injects channel events as user turns, exposes worker tools as an in-process MCP server, keeps the per-session live transcript. Replaced the old worker.ts + WorkerHub WS bridge.

nodes.label: Channel (agent<->backend link)
nodes.rationale: Two-way agent<->backend link. backend->agent: channel events (ticket, answer, prd_validated, nudge, user_comment). agent->backend: worker tool calls.

nodes.label: Protocol (wire format source of truth)
nodes.rationale: Single source of truth for the Channel wire format (tool registry, channel-event union, WS frames) in src/shared/protocol.ts so worker, coordinator, and web client derive from one declaration instead of hand-synced copies. Introduced by architecture candidate #2 (the wire-protocol seam).

nodes.label: Contract (pipeline instructions)
nodes.rationale: Pipeline instructions (contract.ts) injected verbatim as the first ticket channel event. The backend never reasons; the contract tells the agent's own native subagents what to do.

nodes.label: Done Gate
nodes.rationale: Server-side verification of done(pr_url): clean tree, branch pushed, PR exists. The backend does not trust the agent. Failure -> stalled, slot kept.

nodes.label: Coordinator
nodes.rationale: Routes worker tool calls and Stop-hook/turn-end events to state mutations; owns the auto-nudge -> auto-reclaim -> stalled escalation.

nodes.label: Ticket Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Deep module above store (src/server/lifecycle.ts) whose verbs fuse the coupling each transition carries (ticket fields + slot status + finishedAt) with the broadcast/log/notify ritual so a caller can't move stage without syncing slot or forget to push. Architecture candidate #1. Does NOT depend on SessionHub.

nodes.label: System Adapter (side-effect seam)
nodes.rationale: The single side-effect seam (git, tmux, gh, osascript, fs, ~/.claude.json) at src/server/system/. One injectable interface (SystemAdapter) with two implementations.

nodes.label: Triage / Feasibility
nodes.rationale: Read-only implementability analysis run before implementation. Triage runs one session on the real repo; feasibility fans out one batch session over many imported tickets.

nodes.label: Claude Code skills (agent capabilities)
nodes.rationale: Skills installed locally (~/.claude/skills/), most from skillzer. The pipeline contract references specific skills per ticket kind/step; in real mode they must be available or the agent cannot run those steps.

nodes.label: paris-research skill
nodes.rationale: Feature ticket with Research plan enabled: two independent research sub-agents on the approach, then an orchestrator verdict feeding the PRD or implementation framing.

nodes.label: argus (argus-review) skill
nodes.rationale: Feature ticket reviewing step and review ticket: parallel review of the diff or PR (light or full), with optional inline posting on GitHub.

nodes.label: regression-check skill
nodes.rationale: Feature ticket anti-regression step: maps consumers of changed symbols and flags potential regressions.

nodes.label: mockup-fidelity-review skill
nodes.rationale: Feature ticket with functional verification + mockups (Figma links or images): compares the live UI to mockups before opening the PR.

nodes.label: minos-pr-feedback skill
nodes.rationale: Clean ticket: fetches PR review threads, triages by relevance, applies only pertinent fixes.

nodes.label: composer-implement skill
nodes.rationale: Feature ticket with Composer as implementer: delegates code-writing to Cursor headless via templates/run_composer.sh (vendored script, kept in sync with the skill).

nodes.label: Electrobun desktop app (macOS)
nodes.rationale: Packages the app as a macOS desktop application (WebKit). Wrapper starts the Bun backend in-process then opens a window on the frontend. dev:desktop is a real-mode launcher; packaged .app embeds the SDK's native claude binary (claude-bin).

nodes.label: Ticket flow
nodes.rationale: Card created in TODO -> dragged to To implement -> slot (git worktree) acquired and a claude SDK session starts in-process -> agent drives work via MCP tools -> done(pr_url) verified by backend before closing.

nodes.label: Kanban Agents Demo (demo.gif)
nodes.rationale: 52s screen recording walkthrough of the kanban-agents product. Numbered overlay narrates 6 steps: (1) carte creation, (2) ticket detail, (3) feasibility analysis, (5) Claude implements autonomously, (6) automatic review/tests/PR, ending on 'PR #142 ouverte'. Tagline: 'Du ticket a la pull request, sans quitter le tableau.'

nodes.label: Kanban Board (Atelier)
nodes.rationale: Desktop app 'Atelier' with sidebar (Home, Terminals, Stats) and top tabs (Kanban, Agents, Workflow, Worktree). Columns: TODO, A implementer, PRD, A review, Fini. Cards carry project labels (kanban-agents, atelier-web) and a worktree-slots indicator ('4 disponibles, 1 utilises').

nodes.label: New Ticket Modal
nodes.rationale: 'Nouveau ticket' modal with tabs Nouveau ticket / Import CSV / PR Review / PR Cleaner / Ask / Worktree. Fields: Titre, Projet, Lien externe, Branche de base du worktree, Description markdown, Depend du ticket (stack PR). Actions: Annuler, Creer, Creer et lancer.

nodes.label: Agent Implementation Config
nodes.rationale: Per-ticket config: model choice Opus / Sonnet / Haiku, effort level (high), and toggles PRD, PR draft, Auto-merge, Argus multi-loop, Screenshots.

nodes.label: Ticket Detail View
nodes.rationale: Modal with DESCRIPTION / ACTIVITE tabs and DETAILS panel (Branche feat/cost-on-card, Base main, Modele opus-high, Implementeur Claude Code, Faisabilite). Embeds an agent Terminal that appears once the ticket runs in 'A implementer'.

nodes.label: Feasibility Analysis
nodes.rationale: 'Analyser la faisabilite' action sets the Faisabilite field from '-' to 'Implementable' before implementation begins (step 3 of the demo).

nodes.label: Claude Code Autonomous Agent
nodes.rationale: Agent session ('Claude Code - session ticket-a3f9 - slot-2') runs in the embedded terminal, explores code (Read TicketCard.tsx, Grep 'TicketCost'), edits files, and drives the work to completion autonomously (step 5).

nodes.label: Ticket Contract Injection
nodes.rationale: Terminal logs 'Contrat recu - ticket #142 (kind: feature)' showing the ticket spec is injected into the agent session as a contract before work starts.

nodes.label: Stage State Machine
nodes.rationale: Agent emits explicit stage transitions: update_stage -> implementing -> reviewing -> testing -> opening_pr, terminating with done(pr_url). Each transition gates the next action.

nodes.label: Argus Code Review
nodes.rationale: On the reviewing stage the agent 'Lance argus (revue de code en sous-agents)' producing '0 bug bloquant, 1 nit corrige' before tests run.

nodes.label: Automated Tests (typecheck/lint/test)
nodes.rationale: On the testing stage the agent runs 'bun run typecheck', 'bun run lint', 'bun test' (all passing) as an automated quality gate.

nodes.label: Automated Pull Request
nodes.rationale: opening_pr stage pushes 'git push -u origin feat/cost-on-card' and ends with 'done(pr_url) -> PR #142 ouverte'. Closing card celebrates 'PR #142 ouverte'.

nodes.label: Worktree Slots
nodes.rationale: Board header shows 'Slots: 4 disponibles, 1 utilises'; the agent session is bound to 'slot-2'. Each running ticket occupies a worktree slot for parallel isolated execution.

nodes.label: Ticket-to-PR Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Final overlay diagram: PRD -> Worktree -> Implementation -> Revue -> Tests -> PR. Design intent stated as 'Du ticket a la pull request, sans quitter le tableau' (ticket to PR without leaving the board).

nodes.label: PRD Generation
nodes.rationale: A dedicated 'PRD' board column and a 'PRD' toggle in the ticket modal; PRD is the first stage of the end-to-end pipeline overlay.

nodes.label: Dry-run safety model
nodes.rationale: Every external mutation routes through one injectable SystemAdapter interface; FakeSystemAdapter is the default (KANBAN_DRY_RUN!=0) with zero side effects, RealSystemAdapter is never instantiated in the dev/test path so bun run dev is a safe sandbox.

nodes.label: Server-verified done gate
nodes.rationale: done(pr_url) does not trust the agent: the backend re-checks clean working tree, pushed branch, and PR existence via gh pr view; failure marks the ticket stalled and keeps the slot.

nodes.label: Column vs Stage axes
nodes.rationale: COLUMNS (8 board lanes) and STAGES (12 pipeline states) are separate axes; use TERMINAL_STAGES/ACTIVE_STAGES rather than hardcoding.

nodes.label: Config split (config.json vs env)
nodes.rationale: Machine-specific structured config lives in gitignored config.json (zod-validated in config.ts); infrastructure knobs stay in env so project paths never leak back into src/shared.

nodes.label: No type casting convention
nodes.rationale: as/angle-bracket assertions and any are eslint errors (no-restricted-syntax); validate with zod parse/safeParse, narrow with type guards, or use satisfies.

nodes.label: store.ts single DB mutation point
nodes.rationale: store.ts is the only place that mutates the DB and rows.ts zod-validates every row crossing the SQLite boundary, so new fields must be validated there.

nodes.label: Composer 2.5 path (run_composer.sh)
nodes.rationale: Claude plans and delegates code-writing to Cursor headless (run in background and polled), then resumes to review/test/commit/push/PR. Composer never commits — Claude owns all git.

nodes.label: Channel events (ticket/answer/prd_validated/nudge/user_comment)
nodes.rationale: Injected as user turns via a queue-fed async generator — no connect poll, no initialized race, since the contract is simply the first turn.
